title: USD Sublayer Composition Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: The pinned composition, stage_population, and document_data_model specifications are normative. This ledger covers the in-memory recursive-sublayer namespace provider.

status_values.implemented: In scope for the layer-stack namespace-source contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.partial: A bounded subset is included and later contracts must extend it.
status_values.deferred: Specified behavior intentionally not included in this first sublayer contract.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to another subsystem or future skill.

coverage_rules: The sublayer composition contract consumes already-opened Layers; it does not resolve or open sublayer assets.
Recursive stack construction is included only when every reached sublayer has already been supplied at this boundary.
The provider returns a NamespaceSource so existing stage population can consume composed child/property names without learning layer-stack storage.
A generated target must not claim full composition or value resolution from this provider.

obligations: Read root layer subLayers as the direct weaker layer stack.
Pair subLayerOffsets with subLayers for reporting.
Require supplied already-opened layers for authored sublayer assets.
Traverse supplied already-opened layers recursively through authored subLayers.
Insert nested sublayers depth-first immediately after the sublayer that authored them and before later siblings.
Pair each subLayerOffset with the immediate sublayer edge that authored it.
Detect cycles during in-memory stack construction and avoid unbounded recursion.
Do not resolve assets or open resources while constructing the source.
A path exists when any stack layer contributes a spec.
Expose contributing layers in strong-to-weak order.
Expose population-facing fields for stage population.
Preserve weaker def/class definitions through stronger over specs for population query flags.
Return over only when no contributing defining specifier exists.
items.deferred_reason: Full LIVERPS specifier behavior across references, payloads, variants, inherits, specializes, and relocates remains future work.

obligations: Merge primChildren from weakest to strongest contributing spec.
Apply each spec's primOrder after that spec contributes child names.
Do not apply relocates in this first provider.
Union property names across contributing specs.
Sort by path element ordering.
Apply only the strongest authored propertyOrder.

spec_refs: foundational_data_types/README.md:180 list operation supported element types
obligations: Combine same-typed authored listOp field opinions from contributing specs in strong-over-weak order.
Preserve the authored listOp element type in the composed field value.
Do not reinterpret schema semantics or inject schema fallback properties while composing apiSchemas.

spec_refs: resource interface and asset resolution sections
items.deferred_reason: This provider accepts already-opened layers. Resolver policy, resource protocol use, package handling, and unavailable resources remain separate contracts.

obligations: Preserve layer-stack contribution order and subLayerOffsets so the value resolver can apply retiming.
Keep source-visible field storage separate from resolved values.
Route listOp, spline, clip, fallback, and timed value queries through the usd-value-resolution provider.

items.deferred_reason: Relocates affect ordered children and namespace mapping. They are deliberately excluded from this layer-stack provider.
